hormuz.note: The demo spine. Empirically NO terrestrial AISStream coverage as of 2026-07-05 (docs/03 'AIS coverage reality') — kept subscribed at zero cost so it lights up the moment a volunteer receiver appears; its risk density feature reports NO_TERRESTRIAL_COVERAGE instead of a fake zero.

india_west_coast.note: Gujarat approaches + Mumbai — the Indian crude import terminals (Jamnagar, Vadinar, Sikka, Mundra, Mumbai) that Hormuz-transiting cargoes discharge at. Empirically ALSO a coverage void as of 2026-07-05 (diagnostic matrix case G: zero frames, no filter, 180s) — kept subscribed at zero cost like Hormuz; its coverage state reports honestly.

malacca_singapore.note: Singapore Strait — the densest segment of the Malacca corridor named in corridors.json (breadth corridor, not wired for risk in Phase 1). Verified live in the 2026-07-05 diagnostic matrix (case H).
